text: The root cause of the service outage on the XGS-PON tree was excessive optical attenuation on the feeder cable, where a dirty SC/APC connector pushed the received power below minus twenty-eight dBm and drove the ONT out of its optical budget.

The root cause of the service outage on the XGS-PON tree was excessive optical attenuation on the feeder cable, where a dirty SC/APC connector pushed the received power below minus twenty-eight dBm and drove the ONT out of its optical budget.

Field engineers traced the intermittent packet loss to a failing SFP plus on the OLT uplink card, whose degraded transmit power caused CRC errors upstream and repeatedly reset the ONT provisioning through OMCI.

Field engineers traced the intermittent packet loss to a failing SFP plus on the OLT uplink card, whose degraded transmit power caused CRC errors upstream and repeatedly reset the ONT provisioning through OMCI.

After the mass ONT dropout across the street cabinet, the investigation found that a cracked one to thirty-two splitter in the ODN had introduced fluctuating insertion loss, eroding the optical budget on every downstream drop cable.

After the mass ONT dropout across the street cabinet, the investigation found that a cracked one to thirty-two splitter in the ODN had introduced fluctuating insertion loss, eroding the optical budget on every downstream drop cable.

The customer's slow speeds were caused by a VLAN misconfiguration during commissioning, where the wrong service profile was pushed to the ONT, leaving DHCP requests unanswered and blocking upstream traffic on the GPON port.

The customer's slow speeds were caused by a VLAN misconfiguration during commissioning, where the wrong service profile was pushed to the ONT, leaving DHCP requests unanswered and blocking upstream traffic on the GPON port.

Root cause analysis confirmed that water ingress at the ODF had corroded a pigtail near the LC/APC connector, raising attenuation on the distribution cable and forcing the affected ONU offline during periods of high humidity.

Root cause analysis confirmed that water ingress at the ODF had corroded a pigtail near the LC/APC connector, raising attenuation on the distribution cable and forcing the affected ONU offline during periods of high humidity.

The recurring OLT line card alarms were ultimately linked to an overheating chassis in the poorly ventilated cabinet, which caused thermal throttling on the uplink and dropped several XG-PON subscribers during peak afternoon load.

The recurring OLT line card alarms were ultimately linked to an overheating chassis in the poorly ventilated cabinet, which caused thermal throttling on the uplink and dropped several XG-PON subscribers during peak afternoon load.
Engineers determined that a mismatched part number on the replacement SFP module broke the wavelength plan, so the downstream signal fell outside spec and the ONT failed to range against the OLT.
Engineers determined that a mismatched part number on the replacement SFP module broke the wavelength plan, so the downstream signal fell outside spec and the ONT failed to range against the OLT.

The persistent MAC address flapping in the MDU was traced to a loose patch panel connection in the riser, which intermittently broke the fibre continuity and forced repeated re-provisioning of the ONT ID.

The persistent MAC address flapping in the MDU was traced to a loose patch panel connection in the riser, which intermittently broke the fibre continuity and forced repeated re-provisioning of the ONT ID.

During the outage the optical time-domain reflectometer showed a sharp reflection two hundred metres out, confirming that the true root cause was a bend-induced macrobend on the distribution cable rather than an OLT fault.

During the outage the optical time-domain reflectometer showed a sharp reflection two hundred metres out, confirming that the true root cause was a bend-induced macrobend on the distribution cable rather than an OLT fault.

The escalation revealed that a firmware defect on the ONU mishandled OMCI messages after a DHCP lease renewal, dropping the data VLAN and producing the false impression of an upstream fibre break in the access network.

The escalation revealed that a firmware defect on the ONU mishandled OMCI messages after a DHCP lease renewal, dropping the data VLAN and producing the false impression of an upstream fibre break in the access network.
